title: Risk E. Rat's Pizza and Amusement Center

jokes.text: Like, that's where I became a man and saw my first set of boobies, at Risk E.'s.
jokes.setup: Characters discussing what they love about Risk E. Rat's in nostalgic terms
jokes.punchline: Charlie casually mentions seeing breasts at a children's pizza place as a formative experience
jokes.explanation: Strong character moment that establishes Charlie's inappropriate childhood experience as foundational. Good economy and character integration.

jokes.text: Justine the Teen Dream. She was one of the robots in the Animatronic No Rules Band.
jokes.setup: Charlie explaining he saw boobs at a kids' restaurant
jokes.punchline: The 'woman' was an animatronic robot designed for children's entertainment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion that makes the inappropriate childhood experience even more disturbing and absurd. Classic Sunny darkness.

jokes.text: That was the fir-first set of boobs I ever saw since my mom's, so...
jokes.setup: Charlie talking about seeing animatronic boobs
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of having seen his mother's breasts creates uncomfortable overshare
jokes.explanation: Good Charlie character moment with efficient delivery of uncomfortable information. The stutter adds to the awkwardness.

jokes.text: Then you learned your lesson, and they'd send you back out to unleash it on the kids.
jokes.setup: Mac describing the Time-Out Room as a place to 'learn your lesson'
jokes.punchline: The 'lesson' was actually to redirect violence toward other children
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy that reveals the toxic cycle of violence in what should be a children's environment. Good subversion of expectations.

jokes.text: Well, I agree it's total bullshit that we would have to sign a waiver and also that we could bum-rush you and there's nothing you could do about it.
jokes.setup: Employee asking them to sign waivers
jokes.punchline: Mac simultaneously complains about the waiver while threatening the employee
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Mac's contradictory nature - complaining about restrictions while immediately threatening violence.

jokes.text: Frank drinking water from the fountain like an animal
jokes.setup: Characters entering the restaurant normally
jokes.punchline: Frank is discovered drinking inappropriately from a water fountain
jokes.explanation: Visual gag that establishes Frank's animalistic behavior. Simple but effective character moment.

jokes.text: Where he would smack his kids in the head because they were pieces of shit and then he would bust his brother because he was a lazy drunk.
jokes.setup: Mac fondly remembering Risk E.'s comedy routine for kids
jokes.punchline: The 'family-friendly' show involved domestic violence and substance abuse
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy that subverts expectations of children's entertainment. The casual way Mac describes horrific content is perfect.

jokes.text: Yeah, because of their race.
jokes.setup: Dennis dancing around describing racist jokes
jokes.punchline: Mac immediately states the obvious that Dennis is avoiding
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery that cuts through Dennis's verbal gymnastics. Excellent economy and timing.

jokes.text: How do I simulate real-world violence with something like that?
jokes.setup: Employee showing Mac an orange space laser instead of realistic guns
jokes.punchline: Mac openly states his desire to simulate violence with children's toys
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Mac's casual attitude toward violence and his disconnect from normal social expectations.

jokes.text: To get it out of my system, bozo.
jokes.setup: Employee asking why Mac wants to simulate violence
jokes.punchline: Mac treats violence simulation as casual stress relief while insulting the employee
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Mac's casual cruelty. The 'bozo' adds to the childish aggression.

jokes.text: Those were discontinued because cigarettes are illegal.
jokes.setup: Mac asking for candy cigarettes
jokes.punchline: Employee gives completely incorrect reason for their discontinuation
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist moment showing the employee's confusion, though not deeply integrated with character development.

jokes.text: That's what the bone's for! That's what the bone was for!
jokes.setup: Discussing how Risk E. used to hit his head and not wear a helmet
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic recreation of Risk E.'s catchphrase about skull trauma
jokes.explanation: Good nostalgic callback that shows how they fondly remember what would be considered child abuse. Quotable line.

jokes.text: And it's also good for the girls because then they know what they're supposed to look like.
jokes.setup: Dennis arguing that boys seeing animatronic breasts is educational
jokes.punchline: Dennis extends this to claim it's also good for girls as body image guidance
jokes.explanation: Excellent Dennis moment showing his toxic views on women while trying to justify inappropriate content. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: These balls have no weight to 'em. Back in my day, you could crush a man's skull with one of these.
jokes.setup: Frank playing skee-ball
jokes.punchline: Frank nostalgically remembers when arcade balls were lethal weapons
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank line combining nostalgia with violence. The casual delivery of extreme content is expertly crafted.

jokes.text: You can do whatever the fuck you want.
jokes.setup: Child saying he needs to ask his parents about eating candy
jokes.punchline: Frank aggressively tells a child to ignore parental authority
jokes.explanation: Good Frank moment showing his inappropriate influence on children. The profanity adds to the wrongness.

jokes.text: 'Cause you're a jerk.
jokes.setup: Frank being inappropriate with the child
jokes.punchline: Child delivers simple, accurate assessment of Frank's character
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective moment where a child sees through Frank's behavior. Good economy in the delivery.

jokes.text: They used to put 'em on the waitresses without them knowing it... because then you could cop a feel and they couldn't say anything because if they did, they'd lose their jobs.
jokes.setup: Frank explaining how the joke hunt used to work
jokes.punchline: Frank casually describes systematic workplace sexual harassment
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy revealing Frank's casual attitude toward sexual harassment. Perfectly captures the show's willingness to explore terrible behavior.

jokes.text: No, it was the '50s. It was fine.
jokes.setup: Mac saying the sexual harassment was terrible
jokes.punchline: Frank dismisses it with completely inadequate historical context
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank rationalization with excellent economy. The casual dismissal of sexual harassment is darkly funny.

jokes.text: Are they hot, dork? Are you trying to tell me that these sticks of molten cheese that just came out of the deep fryer, they're hot?
jokes.setup: Waitress warning them the mozzarella sticks are hot
jokes.punchline: Mac aggressively mocks her for stating the obvious while being completely over the top
jokes.explanation: Good Mac moment showing his disproportionate anger at minor interactions. The escalation is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Oh, I don't stutter. I have delightful diction.
jokes.setup: Characters expecting Dingbat Duck's signature stutter
jokes.punchline: Duck has been rebranded with pretentious speech pattern instead
jokes.explanation: Good joke about corporate sanitization of potentially offensive characters. The alliteration adds to the pretentiousness.

jokes.text: You d-d-d-d-d-do-do st-st-st-st-stutter.
jokes.setup: Duck claiming he doesn't stutter anymore
jokes.punchline: Mac recreates the stutter while arguing about it
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy moment showing Mac's commitment to proving his point through demonstration. The exaggerated stutter works.

jokes.text: You were the retard.
jokes.setup: Mac arguing about the duck's character
jokes.punchline: Mac casually uses offensive language to describe children's entertainment
jokes.explanation: Character moment showing Mac's insensitivity, though relies somewhat on shock value rather than clever construction.

jokes.text: Well, he's a duck. I wouldn't say that about a human.
jokes.setup: Being told not to use offensive language
jokes.punchline: Mac's logic is that it's okay because the target isn't human
jokes.explanation: Good Mac logic that reveals his bizarre moral reasoning. The distinction he makes is absurdly specific and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Was he supposed to be Black?
jokes.setup: Looking at old animatronic characters
jokes.punchline: Charlie innocently asks about potentially racist characterization
jokes.explanation: Good Charlie moment of innocent observation that highlights uncomfortable truths about older entertainment.

jokes.text: They should have monster voices because we need monster representation.
jokes.setup: Complex discussion about racial representation in purple characters
jokes.punchline: Charlie advocates for monster representation rights
jokes.explanation: Excellent Charlie logic that takes progressive representation ideas to absurd extremes. Perfect character integration with his unique worldview.

jokes.text: This could be science is what you're saying... This is for the good of... future robots, right?
jokes.setup: Dennis and Charlie about to look at robot breasts
jokes.punchline: Dennis desperately rationalizes their inappropriate behavior as scientific research
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis moment showing his need to intellectualize and justify his base desires. Excellent character integration.

jokes.text: They-they took her tits off. They literally sanded 'em off!
jokes.setup: Charlie and Dennis expecting to see robot breasts
jokes.punchline: Discovery that the breasts have been removed in a disturbing way
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that subverts expectations. The violent imagery applied to robot modification is darkly funny.

jokes.text: This is a sad day for women's rights.
jokes.setup: Robot breasts being removed from children's entertainment
jokes.punchline: Dennis frames this as a feminist issue
jokes.explanation: Excellent Dennis moment that perfectly captures his ability to twist any situation into support for his views. Brilliant irony and character work.

jokes.text: You are stupid. It's 'cause you're stupid.
jokes.setup: Child expressing feelings in therapy session
jokes.punchline: Mac immediately insults the child in what should be a safe space
jokes.explanation: Good Mac moment showing his inability to engage appropriately with therapeutic environments or children.

jokes.text: You got no freedom, which means you got no balls... you're never gonna hate yourself enough to stop being bad and grow some balls.
jokes.setup: Mac in therapy session with child
jokes.punchline: Mac delivers toxic masculinity philosophy to a child in therapy
jokes.explanation: Excellent Mac monologue that perfectly encapsulates his toxic worldview. The inappropriateness of delivering this to a child makes it darkly funny.

jokes.text: It gets jalapeño business... You got to do it with an accent. It gets 'all-up-in-yo' business.
jokes.setup: Reading a pun joke about peppers
jokes.punchline: Mac explains the joke requires a racial accent to work
jokes.explanation: Decent cringe moment showing Mac's casual racism in explaining humor. The meta-commentary on offensive jokes works.

jokes.text: But I got to know who the victim is in order for me to laugh at them.
jokes.setup: Discussion about wordplay joke not targeting anyone specific
jokes.punchline: Mac explicitly states he needs a target to find humor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment that exposes Mac's cruel worldview while also being meta-commentary on comedy itself. Excellent writing.

jokes.text: Charlie, there is no bar.
jokes.setup: Charlie suggesting parents should be in the bar
jokes.punchline: Dennis reveals another childhood staple has been removed
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of disappointments about the modernized restaurant. Efficient delivery.

jokes.text: Did he just say Robbie-pizza.com?
jokes.setup: Character performing on stage
jokes.punchline: The beloved mascot now has a corporate website name
jokes.explanation: Good observation about corporate sanitization. The specific absurdity of a website name as a character name works well.

jokes.text: You cannot call a place a website.
jokes.setup: Restaurant apparently renamed to website address
jokes.punchline: Mac's indignation at the corporate naming convention
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor about corporate branding gone wrong. Mac's outrage is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: We recreate the old Risk E.'s so that these kids can have the same experience that we had when we were younger
jokes.setup: Characters disappointed in modernized restaurant
jokes.punchline: Dennis wants to recreate traumatic, inappropriate childhood experiences
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy showing how they romanticize what was actually harmful. The irony of wanting to traumatize children is well-crafted.

jokes.text: Where's my son?! Where's my son?!
jokes.setup: Characters implementing their 'old school' Risk E. experience
jokes.punchline: Panicked parent presumably unable to find child in chaos
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the consequences of their 'improvement' plan. The panic effectively caps the sequence.

jokes.text: They weren't prepared for that amount of fun.
jokes.setup: Chaos and panic in the restaurant
jokes.punchline: Dennis characterizes obvious disaster as too much fun
jokes.explanation: Excellent Dennis rationalization delivered with perfect deadpan. The understatement of obvious chaos is expertly crafted.

jokes.text: Ah, they won't find me.
jokes.setup: Discussion about signed waivers preventing lawsuits
jokes.punchline: Frank reveals he didn't sign anything and plans to disappear
jokes.explanation: Good Frank moment showing his criminal mindset and ability to slip away from consequences. Nice callback structure.
